engineering.translation: Those whose buddhi, ātman, śraddhā, and parāyaṇa reside in That — purified of all faults — reach non-return (apunar-āvṛtti). The verse names a fourfold directional commitment: intellect, self-identification, faith, and refuge, all directed at the supreme. When all four point at the same target, knowledge purifies past faults; the journey terminates. The engineering analog at operational scope: when an engineer's analytical attention, self-conception, professional faith, and operational refuge are all directed at the same destination. Say, the customer's well-being, or the system's integrity, or the team's sustaining loop — the work compounds. The four-fold pointing is structural; partial alignment produces partial purification. STRETCHED honestly because the passage's apunar-āvṛtti exceeds the operating layer.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A principal engineer at a payments company has, across ten years, redirected all four pointers at the same destination: customer-payment-correctness. Her intellectual attention (the Postgres architecture decisions she analyses on the GitHub design-doc) is governed by it. Her self-conception (who she is professionally) is constituted by it; her faith (what she believes will hold under pressure) is invested in it; her refuge (where she goes when the work gets hard) is rooted in it. When a hostile-acquisition-rumour week destabilises the team, she does not collapse — the four pointers are still pointing the same way. When a competitor releases a flashier system, she is not seduced — the four pointers are still pointing the same way. The compounding effect is operationally readable: ten years of the same direction has produced a senior whose contributions to architecture review, code review, on-call. Mentorship all share a recognisable orientation. STRETCHED honestly; apunar-āvṛtti exceeds.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader concludes the four-fold pointing must be at the company's mission statement. The verse's claim is structural — alignment of the four — not corporate. An engineer can have the four pointed at customer-correctness while at a company whose mission statement is something else; the alignment is internal.
engineering.counter_example: When the four pointers are pointed at different destinations — intellect at the customer, self-conception at the promotion, faith in the framework, refuge in the team's social dynamics — the compounding does not happen. The verse names the alignment as the structural condition; partial alignment produces partial result.
engineering.implication: Identify what your four pointers are actually pointing at. Honest answers are rare; aligned answers are rarer. The verse names alignment as the precondition for compounding.
engineering.quotable_line: Intellect, self, faith, refuge. When the four point at the same destination, the work compounds.
